dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The station concourse. A group of residents have gathered around the relay junction access panel — the kind of gathering that means the panel is either broken or they want it to be.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: You're dispatch? Good. The archive's drones cut our water relay node three nights ago. We've been on emergency reserves.

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: I can restore partial function using bypass components. It won't be full capacity, but it'll run until the primary node is recovered.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: We've also got a secondary junction that's been intermittent for months. If you have the parts and the time—

choices.label: Restore the water relay and address the secondary junction [2 resources]
Restore the water relay only — keep moving
Document the situation and continue — you can't spare the resources

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Two hours of work. The water relay comes back online at full capacity. The secondary junction stops intermittent-faulting.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: We'll remember this. If your route comes back through here, there's a standing offer of shelter and supplies.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The water relay comes back at 60% capacity. Enough. The secondary junction will have to wait.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: Understood. Thank you for what you could do.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You log the fault, reference number and timestamp, and explain the situation. The representative's expression doesn't change.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: So we wait.

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: The documentation will help when repair teams come through.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You continue. The intrusion clock ticks forward.

rewards.label: Salvaged Components
rewards.description: The station's maintenance cache — relay parts they can spare.

rewards.label: Network Topology Data
rewards.description: The community's hand-drawn maps of the local relay network — invaluable for what comes next.

rewards.label: Reroute Window
rewards.description: You identify a path that confuses the archive's monitoring sweep, buying {amount} clock units.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Two adjacent communities share a generator hub. The archive AI's salvage drones took one of the two distribution nodes. Now they're arguing over who gets primary access to what remains.

dialogue.speaker: npc-2
dialogue.text: ENGINEER: Medical equipment has priority. I've been saying it for three days.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: And we have three hundred people on pump-dependent water filtration. What's the triage protocol for dehydration?

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: I can't restore the missing node. But I can reconfigure the distribution system to run both loads on the remaining node at reduced efficiency — about 70% capacity each.

dialogue.speaker: npc-2
dialogue.text: ENGINEER: 70% keeps the medical equipment running.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: 70% keeps the filtration pumps running. Do it.

choices.label: Reconfigure the distribution — compromise solution [Knowledge 3 to do this efficiently]
Attempt reconfiguration anyway — you'll make it work
Arbitrate and move on — no time for the technical fix

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The reconfiguration takes twenty minutes. Efficient work. Both communities get their reduced-capacity load. Nobody is happy, but nobody's medical equipment or water filtration is failing.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The reconfiguration works, but it takes longer and costs more components than it should. The distribution holds at about 65% each. Close enough.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You set the distribution to alternate in six-hour blocks. Neither side is satisfied, but neither side is losing essential services. You log the situation and move.

dialogue.speaker: npc-1
dialogue.text: REP: Dispatch will hear about this.

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: I hope so. Tell them what the archive took.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The route continues.

rewards.label: Spare Parts Cache
rewards.description: The engineering cache — components they can spare given what you did for them.

rewards.label: Archive Salvage Logs
rewards.description: A record of exactly what the archive took and when. Useful documentation for arguing scope violation.

rewards.label: Monitoring Blind Spot
rewards.description: The engineer knows the archive's patrol schedule. You gain {amount} clock units by routing around it.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Maintenance junction 7-C. The archive's salvage drones were here recently — fresh scoring on the conduit housing, one relay node missing, another hanging loose by its data cables.

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: The loose node is still partially connected. If I don't secure it, the next salvage pass will take it. If I do secure it, I lose time — and the archive's monitoring system will log the interference.

choices.label: Secure the node and ghost the monitoring log — costs resources but buys time
Secure the node and accept the monitoring flag — clock impact
Document and continue — you need the time more than the node

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You secure the node and run a monitoring ghost — a fake status ping that will hold for about six hours. Long enough for the community downstream to know their junction is stable.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The node is secured. The archive logs a maintenance interference event. You feel the clock accelerate slightly — it's been tracking you since the second stop.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The node will be gone on the next salvage pass. You log it and keep moving. Documentation is better than nothing.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The maintenance junction is behind you.

rewards.label: Recovered Components
rewards.description: Parts from the loose relay node — salvage from salvage.

rewards.label: Salvage Pattern Data
rewards.description: The scoring patterns on the conduit reveal the archive's collection priorities. Useful for building the scope violation argument.

rewards.label: Monitoring Gap
rewards.description: You exploit a latency in the archive's monitoring sweep to gain {amount} clock units.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The facility perimeter begins here. Automated monitoring, salvage drone staging, and — you note — a secondary access corridor the original schematics marked as decommissioned.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: It doesn't look decommissioned. The lock reads as active. The archive repurposed it.

choices.label: Access the secondary corridor — investigate what the archive is using it for [Knowledge 5]
Bypass the perimeter via maintenance access — faster but costs resources
Use primary access — present maintenance credentials and walk in

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The secondary corridor leads to an indexing cache — the archive has been building a physical backup of its network topology maps here. Your current maps versus the archive's maps. The discrepancy is substantial and well-documented.

dialogue.speaker: protagonist
dialogue.text: This is the argument. Seven years of drift, logged and timestamped by the archive's own system.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The maintenance access is clean. You reach the facility interior without triggering the monitoring log. The archive doesn't know you're inside yet.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The archive's systems accept your credentials immediately. Of course they do — you're infrastructure personnel, and you're inside its definition of 'authorized access.' It also logs your entry, and you feel the intrusion clock tick forward.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: The facility interior awaits.

rewards.label: Perimeter Cache
rewards.description: Emergency supplies stored at the perimeter checkpoint — left by whoever last serviced this facility.

rewards.label: Archive Index Data
rewards.description: The archive's own topology records — the discrepancy between its maps and reality, documented in its own format.

rewards.label: Monitoring Override
rewards.description: You locate a monitoring gap in the archive's perimeter sweep. {amount} clock units suppressed.
